intro: Clappertonia minor, also known as "Sea Silk," is a type of marine red algae found in tropical waters. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including skin issues, wounds, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. The bioactive constituents of Clappertonia minor include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
